H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) is the largest HEALING protocol; 66 % positive and strongest YouTube reach (30 views). Listeners report noticeable improvement and typical 10‑min settlement; music overwhelmingly liked/loved. AutoGluon predicts a slightly lower 6.86, suggesting discovery may outpace measured efficacy.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Highly effective for calming overstimulation; listeners report clear improvement and enjoy the music.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) shows 65 % positive. Settlement clusters at 10 min with some immediate responders, indicating bimodal entrainment. Rhythm opinions are split; music neutral for many. YouTube views are low (5).
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Good for overstimulation, though a few find the rhythm a bit busy; most settle in about ten minutes.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) records 64 % positive; listeners often use it for sleep attempts, settling within 5‑10 min. Rhythm balances between comfortable and very settling. YouTube views (8) are modest; drift (0.679) suggests evolving interpretation.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Helps many trying to sleep or work in busy settings; settles quickly for most listeners.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Meadow (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) shows the highest anxiety score; 63 % positive responses and a right‑skewed distribution. Qualitative data (n=23) reports moderate‑to‑significant improvement and typical settlement around 10 min; rhythm is described as balanced. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.84, modestly above the observed mean, suggesting latent potency.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners feel noticeably better after about ten minutes; the rhythm feels calming and balanced.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) yields a tighter spread and 67 % positive responses. Listeners note quicker settlement (≈5 min) but mixed music opinions, with 45 % neutral. No YouTube data. AutoGluon does not flag a large delta for this protocol.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Provides a solid anxiety lift, especially for those who settle quickly, though some find the music just okay.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) maintains solid anxiety relief; 61 % positive. Qualitative responses indicate slower settlement (≈10 min) and a bimodal perception of benefit, hinting at subgroup effects. YouTube views are low (4).
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Works for anxiety but may need a bit longer to feel the benefit; a few users notice little change.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) serves acute distress; 53 % positive. Despite a lower mean, 8 of 24 respondents report significant improvement, and settlement is rapid (≈5 min). No YouTube signals; AutoGluon predicts 7.96 for crisis, higher than observed.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Designed for crisis moments; many report a quick, strong shift toward calm, though not everyone responds the same.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) marginally outperforms Anchor (53 %→55 % positive). Listeners split between neutral and loved music; settlement peaks at 10 min. AutoGluon forecast of 7.96 exceeds the observed mean, flagging a potential efficacy gap.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Some find fast relief in crisis, others take longer; opinions on the music are mixed.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) mirrors Harbor with 52 % positive. Immediate settlement is common (≈37 % within 1 min), yet music dislikes appear, possibly dampening scores. No YouTube data.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Often works within the first minute for many, but a few don’t like the sound and feel less benefit.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong) yields 65 % positive with rapid (≈5 min) settlement. Rhythm described as balanced, though some note “slightly busy.” YouTube views are minimal (4). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, modestly above observed, and drift is highest in the series (0.530).
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Helps people relax and fall asleep quickly; most enjoy the balanced rhythm.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) shows 67 % positive but a distinctive listener mix (40 % client/patient). Rhythm feedback includes “too busy” (17 %). Settlement leans toward 10‑20 min. YouTube views (5) are low; drift (0.681) indicates analytic shift.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Supports sleep for many, but some caregivers notice the rhythm feels too busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) records 64 % positive; settlement typically 5 min. Rhythm opinions split between balanced and slightly busy. YouTube views (3) are minimal; drift (0.611) is moderate.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Works for relaxing and caregiving; most settle within five minutes, though rhythm can feel slightly active.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) records 56 % positive. Listeners cite “slightly busy” rhythm; settlement is quick (5 min or immediate). YouTube views (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.73, a sizable positive delta.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Used for focus; some benefit quickly while others find the rhythm a bit busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful) improves to 66 % positive; settlement leans toward 10 min. Rhythm is balanced; music polarises neutral vs loved. YouTube views (4).
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Improves focus especially in therapy or caregiving contexts; music feedback is mixed.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful) tops THRIVING with 71 % positive. Settlement typically 10 min; rhythm balanced, music liked/loved. YouTube reach highest in THRIVING (15 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.73, very close to observed.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: One of the most effective focus tools; many report big improvements and enjoy the rhythm.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) has 53 % positive. Qualitative notes immediate or 10‑min settlement; music liked/loved. YouTube reach is very low (2). No large AutoGluon delta.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Aids creative focus for some, but results vary; a few feel uplift right away.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful) shows 58 % positive with the tightest variance in the Creative Flow series. Listeners love the music; settlement clusters at 10 min. YouTube views (2) are negligible.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Supports creative flow with a tighter response; many love the music.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) achieves the highest Creative Flow mean; 67 % positive and low variance. Settlement is balanced between 5‑10 min; music opinions mixed. YouTube views (7) are low; drift (0.602) is notable.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Helps creativity strongly for most, with consistent results despite the small sample.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful) shows 64 % positive; settlement often 20 min, reflecting maintenance intent. Rhythm is steady; music opinions split. YouTube views (3).
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Supports balance; some need a longer period to feel its effect.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) has the highest variance in THRIVING (66 % positive). Rhythm opinions are evenly balanced vs slightly busy; settlement quicker (~5 min). YouTube views (4).
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Helps maintain balance but can feel slightly distracting for a few listeners.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing) yields 71 % positive but with a small sample; 8 of 19 report no settlement. Rhythm neutral; music liked. YouTube views (3).
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Generally aids daily balance; a few listeners don’t feel it settle.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) has 54 % positive; AutoGluon predicts a high 8.23, indicating a large unexplained gap. Listeners report mixed music love/neutral; rhythm balanced. YouTube views (8).
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Predicted to be very effective for low energy, but observed benefits are moderate.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) marginally better (64 % positive). Settlement both fast and slow; music liked. YouTube views (8). AutoGluon predicts high efficacy, reinforcing a potential under‑discovery.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Helps mood and energy; reactions range from quick help to slower settling.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) shows 56 % positive; some report no effect. Rhythm split between very calming and balanced. YouTube views (4).
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Provides modest mood lift; some listeners report no effect.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) shows only 45 % positive. Rhythm mostly balanced; settlement quick for many. YouTube views (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.88, a substantial positive delta.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Intended for motivation; only a minority feel a strong lift.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) records 43 % positive; some listeners require >20 min to settle. Rhythm slightly busy for several. YouTube views (7).
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Motivation support is mixed; some need a longer time to settle.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) yields 62 % positive but with bimodal distribution. Rhythm feedback mixed; settlement varies. YouTube views (7). AutoGluon does not list a prediction; drift is extreme (0.507).
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Results vary widely; some feel energized while others notice little change.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) records 54 % positive; two respondents note worsening clarity. Settlement occurs within 5‑10 min; music well‑liked. YouTube views (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, modestly above observed.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Aims for mental clarity; most notice improvement, though a couple feel worse.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing) shows 56 % positive; strong love for music. Fast settlement for half the sample. YouTube views (12) are highest in this sub‑line. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a notable uplift.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Fast‑acting for clarity; many love the music and feel calmer quickly.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) is the lowest performer (50 % positive) with high variance. Listeners report rhythm as too busy; settlement often absent. YouTube views (8) but efficacy lags. AutoGluon prediction not provided; analysts flag as anomaly.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Underperforms; listeners find the rhythm busy and many don’t feel any benefit.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset shows moderate‑to‑high outcome scores with distinct patterns across the three series.
• HEALING delivers the most consistent results (lowest variance) and strong scores for DESCENT protocols.
• THRIVING scores are comparable to HEALING but show clear tiered gains in Focus & Clarity.
• TRANSFORMING exhibits the widest spread and lower means, reflecting its ASCENT design for low‑affect states.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Each series manifests its therapeutic intent through characteristic efficacy signatures.
• HEALING’s DESCENT arcs produce stable, high‑scoring outcomes across anxiety, overstimulation, and sleep categories.
• THRIVING’s HOLD protocols yield a dose‑response pattern, especially in Focus & Clarity where Level 3 excels.
• TRANSFORMING’s ASCENT arcs show greater variability; some protocols underperform while others (e.g., X‑ME‑L1) have high predicted potential.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A handful of protocols diverge from series norms, meriting focused inquiry.
• X‑MC‑L3 (Cosmos) records the lowest mean (5.7) and high variance, signaling possible mismatch.
• X‑MD‑L1/L2 display unusually low positive‑response rates (45 %/43 %) despite ASCENT intent.
• H‑SL‑L2 shows a bimodal listener mix (clinical vs self) and rhythm feedback flagged as “too busy.”
• T‑MB‑L2 presents the highest standard deviation in THRIVING (2.03), indicating inconsistent efficacy.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted refinements emerging from convergent analyst observations.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MD‑L1 & X‑MD‑L2 – weak positive response; test shorter hold phases or alternative frequency ramps.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MC‑L3 – under‑performing; explore L1/L2 routing, increase duration, or reassess target audience.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑MB‑L2 – high variance; prototype a calmer rhythm profile and evaluate impact.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑OS‑L1 – strong outcome plus highest YouTube reach; analyze discoverability features to replicate across the library.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑ME‑L1 – AutoGluon predicts 8.23 vs observed 6.74; boost adviser routing and visibility.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑SL‑L1 – notable drift; monitor sample composition and consider stabilising advisory guidance.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑FC‑L3 – high performance with drift; investigate consistency of qualitative feedback and possible cohort effects.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑CR‑L2 – predicted 7.96 vs observed 6.52; review crisis‑state onboarding and music alignment.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift and clustering provide complementary context.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 29 of 30 protocols show similarity < 0.85, indicating evolving analytic framing as data accrues.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Tight pairs include T‑FC‑L1↔T‑FC‑L2, H‑SL‑L1↔H‑SL‑L2, T‑CF‑L1↔T‑CF‑L2; most isolated are H‑OS‑L3, T‑FC‑L3, T‑CF‑L3.
• Confidence note: Drift derived from ~1 k rows and historic token embeddings; signals are meaningful but should be interpreted with moderation.
